text: And we're extinguishing and getting deeper in there. We're trying to get a couple hundred feet into that control line, mopping up around those structures. As we get in here to Panga Canyon area, we did have some concerns last night. We did have a little bit of a slop over. You'll see right in here in the Z on the map, that Division Zulu fire started to approach down, and we had some concerns in the Fernwood area. We were able to mitigate some of those situations last night, and it was looking really well. So overall progression with the fire crews yesterday, we did really well. One of the things I'd like to remind all the residents: UAS operations. So when we're talking about drones on the fire, any time that a civilian puts up a drone in our airspace, we're unable to fly our aircrafts. So if you're flying, it means we can't. So we're trying to do our job out there. So I appreciate everybody respect the the no drone flights in the fire area. That'll conclude our operational briefing for today.
